items.title: Monitor droplet resources for main and Study Coach
items.context: Cron migration moved business-critical automations onto the main droplet and Study Coach droplet. Zoom transcript ingestion also stores raw transcript text on disk, so disk, inode, memory, and service-resource issues could break operations before an individual job alert explains the root cause.
items.notes: Completed 2026-07-15. Implemented Cron Dashboard V2 infrastructure health monitoring for main and Study Coach droplets: disk, inodes, memory, swap, load, failed units, monitored service state, journald size, allowlisted directory sizes, latest/history storage, dashboard section, and dashboard-only resource alerts. Revisit after 24h/7d trend validation before enabling Telegram alerts.

items.title: Put cron monitor and dashboard deployment under version control
items.context: Cron Dashboard V2 and the central monitor were built during the migration and patched directly on the main droplet. The monitor now aggregates main droplet, Study Coach, and OpenClaw status, powers Telegram/email alerts, and is monitored by UptimeRobot.
items.notes: Completed 2026-07-15. Droplet-side Cron Dashboard V2 backend source, systemd unit, env template, deploy script, verify script, and README are now versioned in molodious/mpep-droplet under ops/cron-dashboard-v2 at commit d4b0de6. Real env values and runtime state remain outside git. No production restart or redeploy was performed.

items.title: Quiet Exam Good Luck Scheduler routine emails
items.context: After migration to the main droplet, the Exam Good Luck Scheduler sent a routine weekly email even when no good-luck broadcasts were scheduled and no cleanup action happened. Dan wants this job to run silently when there is no action.
items.notes: Audited 2026-07-15. Live scheduler already skips email on pure no-action runs. It sends only when broadcasts are created, expired tags are cleaned up, cleanup errors occur, or the job fails. Current behavior is acceptable, so no implementation change planned.

items.title: Decide long-term token health check strategy
items.context: OpenClaw still has a Token Health Check for Google, Kit, HubSpot, Zoom, and Typeform. During migration we decided direct token monitoring may be optional because central job-health monitoring will often reveal stale-token failures indirectly.
items.notes: Completed 2026-07-15. Audited token-dependent automations across main droplet, Study Coach, OpenClaw, and relevant GitHub Actions. Retired the OpenClaw Token Health Check scheduled job and kept the sanitized OpenClaw workspace backup enabled. Rely on real job-health monitoring from migrated droplet and Study Coach automations rather than broad token heartbeat checks. Add narrow direct token checks only if a future incident exposes a real blind spot.

items.title: Add CRM data-quality flags surfaced by student briefs
items.context: The new candidate/student briefing workflow exposed CRM data-quality issues, such as enrolled students marked as cold leads. Brief quality depends on accurate enrollment status, program, discipline, and interaction history.
items.notes: Won't do 2026-07-15. Brief quality issues should be handled through manual review of actual briefs and targeted CRM/source-data correction, rather than broad proactive flagging that is likely to be noisy or speculative. If a brief exposes a simple issue, correct the CRM source record directly; if complex, ask Codex to audit that specific contact, brief, or transcript and recommend the source-data correction.

items.title: Create unmatched Zoom transcript review queue
items.context: Zoom transcript ingestion downloads all transcript text but only summarizes and stores CRM interactions when the transcript can be matched to a CRM contact. Some calls may be vendors, friends, group sessions, or legitimate students/prospects missing from CRM.
items.notes: Won't do 2026-07-17. If Dan is speaking with someone on Zoom, he normally has their email, which means they should already be a CRM contact. Handle any rare unmatched transcript case ad hoc rather than maintaining a separate review queue.

items.title: Document final cron automation architecture and runbook
items.context: The cron migration moved automations across main droplet, Study Coach droplet, and OpenClaw, with Cron Dashboard V2 now acting as the operations board and central alerting layer. The final state is clear in the dashboard but not yet captured as a durable runbook.
items.notes: Completed 2026-07-17. Covered by the canonical system architecture/runbook and the BL-004 recovery documentation work: current automation locations, Cron Dashboard V2 role, alerting, monitoring, and recovery/debug context are now documented well enough for operations.

items.title: Hardcoded secrets in cron payloads backed up to GitHub
items.context: GMAIL_APP_PASSWORD and KIT_V4_API_KEY are hardcoded in Openclaw cron payloads that get backed up to GitHub nightly. Secret leakage risk.
items.notes: Completed 2026-07-15. Replaced raw OpenClaw workspace backup with sanitized metadata-only backup. Removed tracked raw cron/config exports from backup repo, added secret scan, re-enabled weekly backup cron, and verified current ace-workspace backup contains only sanitized metadata. Historical exposure accepted without rotating credentials.

items.title: Stripe manual invoice script not yet deployed
items.context: scripts/create-stripe-invoice.js written to handle future manual invoices but not yet deployed.
items.notes: Superseded 2026-07-15. Manual invoice creation now flows through the Telegram invoice/Admin-Ops bot; invoice-bot payments are handled by the sales webhook and logged automatically. The old scripts/create-stripe-invoice.js deployment is no longer needed. Optional separate security review: public checkout/invoice endpoints should not allow arbitrary amount or product abuse.

items.title: Disaster recovery — commit remaining droplet config surface
items.context: Service code, ecosystem.config.js, and package files are now committed. Need to close remaining recovery gaps.
items.notes: Done 2026-07-16. DigitalOcean backups confirmed for both droplets. Private GitHub recovery package created in molodious/mpep-droplet with RECOVERY.md, sanitized inventories, collector/verifier scripts, weekly automated inventory publishing to recovery/weekly-inventory, and Cron Dashboard monitoring.

items.title: Disaster recovery — write RECOVERY.md runbook
items.context: No documented end-to-end recovery procedure exists. Forces discovery of unknown unknowns by writing it down.
items.notes: Won't do 2026-07-16. Superseded by BL-004, which now covers both the droplet recovery package/config-surface work and the RECOVERY.md/runbook deliverable for main and Study Coach droplets.

items.title: FE testimonials and pass rate data
items.context: fe.html lacks FE-specific student quotes and pass rate data. Sample size too small to support a defensible claim.
items.notes: Someday 2026-07-16. Keep as a Product Proof opportunity. Revisit when FE sample size grows enough for defensible testimonials or pass-rate proof; no active implementation until enough FE completions/pass reports exist.

items.title: Hook exam dashboard rebuilds into kit-webhook approval flow
items.context: Exam dashboard currently rebuilds once daily at 6am via scheduler. Near-real-time updates would surface results faster.
items.notes: Completed 2026-07-16. Audited code, production droplet configuration, and GitHub Actions history. The deployed kit webhook triggers molodious/mpep-dashboard repository_dispatch event exam-date-updated after exam_date updates, production webhook/.env contains GITHUB_PAT, and recent workflow runs confirm successful repository_dispatch rebuilds. No new implementation needed.

items.title: Verify sessions_send sessionKey format
items.context: SessionKey format not yet verified against a live test.
items.notes: Won't do 2026-07-17. Audit found this belonged to an old OpenClaw Telegram delivery path. Current production jobs no longer use sessions_send or sessionKey; no active dependency found in code or logs. Original live-test action is obsolete after migration to droplet/systemd jobs.

items.title: Disable dead Stripe webhook endpoint
items.context: Webhook we_1TDkZmLeBhBRYzk45c2gbSiA points to unreachable URL. Stripe is retrying dead URL.
items.notes: Completed 2026-07-17. Read-only Stripe API audit confirmed dead endpoint we_1TDkZmLeBhBRYzk45c2gbSiA is no longer present. Remaining enabled endpoints point to current btc.mechanicalpeexamprep.com routes: /stripe-webhook-sales and /stripe-webhook. No Stripe action needed.

items.title: Node.js 20 deprecation in GitHub Actions
items.context: GitHub Actions workflow will break June 2, 2026 when Node 20 actions are deprecated.
items.notes: Completed 2026-07-17. Audited local MPEP GitHub Actions workflows and found Node 20 action risk limited to mpep-dashboard. Updated dashboard workflows to Node 24-compatible action versions: checkout@v5, setup-python@v6, configure-pages@v6, and upload-pages-artifact@v5. Droplet and kit-webhook workflows use Docker-based ssh-action and did not need changes.

items.title: mpep-dashboard repo is public — assess risk and consider mitigation
items.context: molodious/mpep-dashboard is public. OH prep content and any hardcoded values are visible via GitHub search.
items.notes: Updated 2026-07-17. IP exposure substantially remediated: OH Prep generated problem pages, solution folders, and OH Prep JSON/index data were removed from the public dashboard repo and moved behind Study Coach droplet auth. CRM Phase 1 is deployed on the main droplet behind Basic Auth with shared username mpep: public CRM pages are now safe moved stubs, the dashboard card points to https://internal.mechanicalpeexamprep.com/crm/, the browser no longer receives the CRM bearer token, and the formerly public CRM token has been rotated. Implementation lesson for future dashboard moves: use the internal.mechanicalpeexamprep.com protected route pattern and strip the old client-side dashboard password overlay/auth.js from server-protected pages so users do not see a second broken password prompt. Keep open for remaining work: migrate sales/financial/accountable-plan/tax-planner/scorecard/exams/pass-rates/cron/backlog behind the same protected internal-dashboard pattern, and remove/rotate the sales/orders shared-secret URL.

items.title: Telegram alert on backup failure
items.context: Weekly app_backup.sh cron logs 'Push FAILED' but sends no alert. Silent failure mode.
items.notes: Won't do 2026-07-17. This referred to the legacy Sunday /root/app_backup.sh runtime-data push, which logs Push FAILED but does not send Telegram directly. Current posture relies on DigitalOcean weekly droplet backups, the recovery inventory publisher, and periodic cron dashboard review rather than bolting direct Telegram alerting onto the old script.

items.title: Backup cadence — daily vs weekly
items.context: Up to 7 days of sales_webhook_log.jsonl and fulfilled-orders.json data could be lost between weekly runs.
items.notes: Decision updated during cron migration: OpenClaw workspace backup intentionally runs weekly on Sundays because it backs up the OpenClaw workspace itself. Revisit only if the weekly loss window becomes unacceptable.

items.title: Monitor sales_webhook_log repo size growth
items.context: File grows over time; git history accumulates the full file on every weekly commit.
items.notes: Check repo size periodically. Consider git-LFS or alternate backup strategy if it crosses ~50MB.

items.notes: Verify completion status.

items.notes: Confirm current state. If WordPress is hosted there, this is closed. If not, consider canceling.

items.title: Paddle.net reviews plugin verification

items.notes: Confirm whether Paddle.net is actively used. If not, cancel the subscription.

items.title: cron_health.sh is dormant
items.context: Watchdog script exists but is not scheduled in crontab and has never run. Dead code.
items.notes: Superseded by Cron Dashboard V2 and the central cron monitor, which now aggregate job status across main droplet, Study Coach, and OpenClaw and provide Telegram/email alerting.

items.title: Backup invoice bot to GitHub
items.context: Invoice bot code, ecosystem.config.js, package files committed to molodious/mpep-droplet.
items.notes: Commit dad0657.

items.title: Updated mpep-droplet-backup.md to clarify scope
items.context: Doc now distinguishes cron-backed-up files from manually-committed code.
items.notes: Added Disaster Recovery Gaps section.

items.title: DigitalOcean droplet backups enabled
items.context: ~$1-2/mo. Closes ~90% of disaster recovery gap. Single biggest recovery improvement.
items.notes: Enabled in DO dashboard.

items.title: .env stored in password vault
items.context: Off-droplet copy of all secrets. Closes the secret-recovery gap.
items.notes: Stored in password manager.
